history: The patient was admitted with a complaint of melena. Abdominal computed tomography revealed a dense mass and a target sign in the distal ileum, suggesting an intestinal mass and associated intussusception. However, there were no signs of intestinal obstruction or ischemic change. Retrograde single-balloon enteroscopy revealed a lesion in the distal ileum that appeared as a spherical mass in the frontal view, with a long stalk visible posteriorly. Enteroscopic clipping and tattooing were performed for preoperative marking.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Abdominal computed tomography revealed a dense mass and a target sign in the distal ileum. Retrograde single-balloon enteroscopy revealed a lesion in the distal ileum. Contrast examination revealed a long and slender mass with a hemispherical, dome-shaped head.
correct_diagnosis: Enteric muco-submucosal elongated polyp (EMSEP) with intussusception
differentials: Enteric muco-submucosal elongated polyp (EMSEP) with intussusception
correct_ranking: Enteric muco-submucosal elongated polyp (EMSEP) with intussusception
